notes: Experiments designed for local GPU cluster (8x RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell, 96GB VRAM). LeWM is 15M params — fits comfortably on a single GPU. max_gpus=2 per config. Phase 0 (setup) must complete before any experiment tasks. Pilot tasks establish framework validity before full runs.

tasks.name: Environment and Dependency Setup
tasks.description: Install le-wm (GitHub: lucas-maes/le-wm), stable-world-model, dm-control, mujoco 3.x, peft, necessary-compositionality, and scikit-learn. Create conda env sibyl_lewm-generalization. Verify Walker environment renders correctly with custom physics parameters (gravity, friction, density). Verify le-wm training script runs for 1 step without error.

tasks.name: Collect ComPhys-LeWM Benchmark Data (27 Factor Combinations)
tasks.description: Generate 200 trajectories per factor combination using scripted random-walk policy in DMControl Walker-walk. Factor space: gravity {0.5g, 1.0g, 2.0g} x friction {0.5x, 1.0x, 2.0x} x body_density {0.5x, 1.0x, 2.0x} = 27 combos. Store as HDF5 with: 64x64 RGB frames, joint angles (DoF), CoM velocity, contact forces, physics parameter labels. Output: 27 HDF5 files in exp/data/comphys_lewm/. Also generate the 3 CoGenT-style training/holdout splits (18/27 train, 9/27 holdout each) and save as exp/data/splits.json.

pilot.description: Collect 100 trajectories for 3 friction combos (0.5x, 1.0x, 2.0x) only (gravity=1.0g, mass=1.0x). Verify HDF5 format, data shape, and labels are correct.
pilot.pass_criteria: HDF5 files readable; frame shape (100, T, 64, 64, 3); physics labels stored correctly; no NaN frames

tasks.name: Pilot P1: Framework Validation (Friction Holdout, 1 Seed)
tasks.description: Train LeWM-SIGReg on friction levels {0.5x, 2.0x} in Walker (gravity=1.0g, mass=1.0x fixed). Use exactly 100 trajectories per training combo (pilot constraint). Probe with linear regression on: (a) friction=1.0x (interpolation), (b) friction=3.0x (extrapolation, generate 100 additional trajectories). Report linear probing R² for in-distribution vs. interpolation vs. extrapolation. Decision rule: if R² drops <5% relative on interpolation → use more extreme holdout in full study; if >15% drop → single-factor generalization is already challenging; if >40% drop → reduce factor range. Also run pilot P2: apply LoRA-r4 to predictor Q/V; fine-tune on 50 trajectories from friction=1.0x holdout; measure R² recovery.

pilot.description: Train LeWM on 2 friction levels x 100 trajectories each; probe on 100 interpolation + 100 extrapolation trajectories; LoRA-r4 fine-tune on 50 trajectories
pilot.pass_criteria: Training loss converges (final < 0.1 initial); linear probe R² > 0.1 in-distribution (sanity check); framework computes without error

visualizations.description: Probing R2 comparison: in-distribution vs. interpolation vs. extrapolation

tasks.name: Train LeWM-SIGReg Primary Model (7 Seeds, 18/27 Training Combos)
tasks.description: Train LeWM with SIGReg regularizer on the primary 18/27 CoGenT training split (seed_split_0). 7 random training seeds {42, 7, 13, 21, 99, 137, 256}. Hyperparameters: 200 epochs, batch_size=64, AdamW lr=1e-4, SIGReg λ=0.1 (default). Save all 7 checkpoints to exp/results/full/lewm_sigreg_primary/. NOTE: 7 sequential training runs of ~4h each = ~28h total; break into 2 parallel batches of 3-4 seeds each to use 2 GPUs concurrently (within max_gpus=2 budget).

pilot.description: 1-seed 5-epoch training on 100-trajectory subset; verify loss curves stable
pilot.pass_criteria: Training loss decreasing; no NaN; checkpoint saves correctly

tasks.name: Train LeWM-VICReg (3 Seeds, Same Split)
tasks.description: Train LeWM with VICReg regularizer (replace SIGReg with VICReg invariance-variance-covariance loss) on the same primary 18/27 training split. 3 seeds {42, 7, 13}. Same 200-epoch regime. Save checkpoints to exp/results/full/lewm_vicreg/. Tests H2: does SIGReg promote more orthogonal factorization than VICReg?

pilot.pass_criteria: VICReg loss converges (3 terms: invariance, variance, covariance all decreasing); no collapse

tasks.name: Train LeWM-NoReg (3 Seeds, Collapse Control)
tasks.description: Train LeWM without any regularizer (remove SIGReg and VICReg). Expected to potentially collapse (degenerate solutions). 3 seeds {42, 7, 13}. 200 epochs. Save checkpoints to exp/results/full/lewm_noreg/. Used as control: if no-reg also shows low holdout drop, the regularizer is necessary for generalization; if no-reg collapses, confirms SIGReg/VICReg necessity.

pilot.description: 1-seed 5-epoch no-regularizer training; monitor embedding variance collapse
pilot.pass_criteria: Training proceeds without crash; log embedding variance at epoch 1 and 5 for collapse detection

tasks.name: Train Oracle (All 27 Combos) and Matched-Volume Control
tasks.description: Two baseline training runs: (A) Oracle: LeWM-SIGReg trained on all 27 factor combinations (ceiling performance, no holdout). (B) Matched-volume control: LeWM-SIGReg trained on 18 randomly-selected combos (no holdout structure, same data volume as primary). Both with 3 seeds {42, 7, 13}. Outputs to exp/results/full/lewm_oracle/ and exp/results/full/lewm_matched_vol/.

pilot.description: 1-seed 5-epoch oracle training on all 27 combos, 100-traj subset
pilot.pass_criteria: Training loss lower than primary at same epoch (sanity check for more training data)

tasks.name: Train DINO-WM (3 Seeds, Same Split)
tasks.description: Train DINO-WM (frozen DINOv2-s encoder + learned transformer predictor) on the same primary 18/27 split. 3 seeds. Evaluation on holdout combos. Architecture comparison: does DINO-WM show same generalization failure pattern as LeWM (suggesting the issue is in the predictor / data, not the specific encoder)?

pilot.description: 1-seed 5-epoch DINO-WM training; verify DINOv2 encoder frozen and predictor trains correctly
pilot.pass_criteria: DINOv2 encoder frozen (no grad update on encoder params); predictor loss decreases; model runs forward pass on held-out combo without error

tasks.name: Probing Evaluation: H1 Compositional Generalization Gap
tasks.description: Run Level 1 probing evaluation on all trained models. For each model checkpoint (7 seeds for LeWM-SIGReg, 3 seeds for others): (1) extract frozen encoder embeddings on ALL 27 combos; (2) train linear regression probe (sklearn Ridge, alpha=1e-3) to predict: joint angles, CoM velocity, gravity label, friction label, mass label; (3) evaluate R² on in-distribution combos and holdout combos separately; (4) compute relative R² drop = (R²_in_dist - R²_holdout) / R²_in_dist. Primary metrics: mean R² drop across holdout combos, per-factor-label R² drop. Statistical tests: paired t-test (in-dist vs. holdout) across 7 seeds; bootstrap 95% CI; Cohen's d. Save results to exp/results/full/probing_h1_results.json.

pilot.description: Probe on pilot_framework_validation checkpoint; verify R² scores are non-trivial and output JSON schema is correct
pilot.pass_criteria: R² in-distribution > 0.3 for at least one target; JSON output contains fields: model, seed, in_dist_r2, holdout_r2, relative_drop for each target

visualizations.description: Table 1: Main results — probing R2 and CEM success rate (in-distribution vs. holdout) for all model variants
Figure 1: Heatmap of compositional generalization gap across 9 holdout combinations (factor grid)

tasks.name: Geometric Analysis: H2 (SIGReg vs. VICReg Orthogonality) and H4 (Displacement Consistency)
tasks.description: Run Level 2 geometric analysis on LeWM-SIGReg vs. LeWM-VICReg checkpoints (3 seeds each): (1) Per-factor latent subspace PCA: extract top-8 PCs from embeddings grouped by each factor level; (2) Principal angle analysis using necessary-compositionality codebase: compute principal angles between per-factor subspaces; report mean cosine similarity; (3) Displacement vector consistency (parallelogram test): compute centroid delta-vectors at ≥3 reference points per factor; compute cosine consistency score; (4) CKA between factor-grouped representations as secondary metric. Regression analysis for H4: OLS with holdout R² as outcome; compare R² with principal angles alone vs. principal angles + displacement consistency (incremental R² test). Save to exp/results/full/geometric_h2_h4_results.json.

pilot.description: Run principal angle analysis on pilot checkpoint; verify necessary-compositionality runs; output principal angle matrix for 3 factors
pilot.pass_criteria: Principal angle matrix (3x3) computed; mean cosine similarity in [0, 1]; no import errors from necessary-compositionality

visualizations.description: Figure 2a: Principal angle matrix SIGReg vs. VICReg (3x3 factor pairs, cosine similarity)
Figure 2b: Displacement vector consistency vs. holdout R2 scatter (H4)

tasks.name: Interventional Independence Score (IIS): H3 Predictor Bottleneck and H2b
tasks.description: Run Level 3 interventional probing on LeWM-SIGReg primary model (3 seeds). For each test trajectory and each physical concept pair (C, D): (1) Encode trajectory to latent sequence; (2) Identify concept-C direction from linear probe; (3) Gram-Schmidt orthogonalize probe directions to avoid correlation confounds; (4) Shift latents by δ along concept-C direction (corresponding to 1-step factor change); (5) Roll out through predictor; (6) Compute IIS(C,D) = 1 - |corr(intervention_on_C, change_in_D_prediction)|. Compute full 3x3 IIS matrix for (gravity, friction, mass). Baseline: repeat with random shift direction. Compute: Pearson r(IIS, principal_angles), Pearson r(IIS, holdout R²), Pearson r(IIS, CEM success rate). Save to exp/results/full/iis_h3_results.json.

pilot.description: Compute IIS for gravity-friction pair on 100 pilot trajectories; verify random-direction baseline yields IIS ~0.5
pilot.pass_criteria: IIS(gravity, friction) computed; random baseline IIS in [0.4, 0.6]; no numerical overflow/underflow

visualizations.description: Table 2: IIS matrix (3x3 concept pairs) with random-direction baseline comparison

tasks.name: LoRA Adaptation Diagnostic: H3, H3a (Encoder vs. Predictor Bottleneck)
tasks.description: Run LoRA adaptation experiments on held-out combos (9 combos from primary split), starting from LeWM-SIGReg best checkpoint (seed 42). Adaptation targets: (a) encoder Q/V only, (b) predictor Q/V only, (c) both encoder+predictor. LoRA ranks: {2, 4, 8, 16}. Adaptation data sizes: {10, 25, 50, 100, 200} trajectories from target domain. Metrics: probing R² recovery (relative to in-distribution), CEM success rate, samples-to-90%-recovery. Baselines: (0) zero-shot, (1) head-only fine-tuning, (2) full fine-tuning (ceiling), (3) random untrained LoRA. In-distribution LoRA control: same adaptation on one in-distribution combo (verify it doesn't trivially help). If LoRA underperforms (rank ≤16 insufficient), extend ranks to {32, 64}. Save all results to exp/results/full/lora_adaptation_results.json.

pilot.description: LoRA-r4 predictor-only; 50 trajectories from single held-out combo; measure R² recovery. This is pilot P2 from hypotheses.md.
pilot.pass_criteria: LoRA adapter inserts without error; fine-tuning converges (loss decreases over 20 steps); R² recovery computed and reported

visualizations.description: Figure 3: LoRA adaptation curves — R2 recovery vs. trajectories (encoder / predictor / both) by LoRA rank

tasks.name: CEM Planning Evaluation: Behavioral Generalization (Level 4)
tasks.description: Run CEM (Cross-Entropy Method) planning in latent space on LeWM-SIGReg (7 seeds) for in-distribution and holdout combos. Task: Walker-walk reach target joint configuration. 50 trajectories per holdout combo per seed. Metrics: success rate (%), latent prediction MSE. Compute: relative CEM success rate drop (in-dist vs. holdout). Compare with DINO-WM CEM success rate on same split. Note: CEM planning is purely in latent space; no additional environment rollout needed. Save to exp/results/full/cem_planning_results.json.

pilot.description: CEM planning on pilot checkpoint; 10 trajectories on 1 in-distribution and 1 holdout combo; verify success rate computation
pilot.pass_criteria: CEM planning runs without error; success rate in [0, 1]; in-distribution SR >= 0.0 (even if low on small pilot)

tasks.name: Physical Regime Analysis: H5 (Physical Coupling Predicts Regime-Dependent Failure)
tasks.description: CPU-based analysis of physical coupling between factors. (1) From trajectory data, compute summary statistics S per combination: velocity variance, contact frequency, energy dissipation proxy (e.g., ||joint_velocity||^2 change per step). (2) Estimate coupling strength for each factor pair (f_i, f_j) using finite-difference mixed partial derivative: c(i,j) = |S(hi,hj) - S(hi,lj) - S(li,hj) + S(li,lj)| / (Δfi * Δfj). (3) Classify each holdout combo as weakly/strongly coupled using threshold c* = median coupling strength. (4) Test: does coupling strength predict holdout probing R² drop (from eval_probing_h1) better than Grassmannian distance? Partial regression. (5) Check concordance: do strongly-coupled failures persist across LeWM-SIGReg, LeWM-VICReg, DINO-WM? Save to exp/results/full/regime_analysis_h5_results.json.

pilot.description: Compute coupling strength for friction-gravity pair from pilot data (100 trajectories per combo); verify finite-difference computation is numerically stable
pilot.pass_criteria: Coupling strength matrix (3x3 factor pairs) computed; values non-negative; no NaN; output JSON has correct schema

visualizations.description: Figure 4: Coupling strength vs. holdout failure rate with regression line; multi-model concordance panel

tasks.name: SIGReg Lambda Sweep (H2a): In-Distribution vs. Holdout Optima
tasks.description: Train LeWM-SIGReg with 4 lambda values: {0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2}. 3 seeds each. Primary split (18/27). Measure in-distribution probing R² and holdout probing R² for each lambda. Plot dual-axis: in-dist vs. holdout performance as a function of lambda. Expected: optimal lambda for holdout > optimal lambda for in-dist. Save to exp/results/full/sigreg_lambda_sweep_results.json.

pilot.description: Train 2 lambda values (0.01 and 0.2) for 5 epochs on 100-trajectory subset; verify lambda affects training loss differently
pilot.pass_criteria: Lambda sweep runs for both values; training loss at epoch 5 differs between lambda=0.01 and lambda=0.2 by >5%

visualizations.description: H2a: In-distribution vs. holdout R2 as function of SIGReg lambda

tasks.name: Split Sensitivity Analysis (3 CoGenT Splits)
tasks.description: Evaluate the sensitivity of H1 results to the choice of CoGenT training/holdout split. Re-run eval_probing_h1 on LeWM-SIGReg checkpoints trained on splits 1 and 2 (3 seeds each), using the data already collected. Measure variance in relative R² drop across splits. This validates that the compositional generalization gap is robust to split choice, not an artifact of one particular holdout selection.

pilot.description: Train 1 seed on split 1 (100-trajectory subset) and run probing; verify different holdout R² than split 0
pilot.pass_criteria: Training and probing completes for split 1; R² values saved correctly with split label

visualizations.description: H1 relative R2 drop across 3 splits (mean ± std)

tasks.name: Results Synthesis and Paper-Ready Tables/Figures
tasks.description: Aggregate all experiment results into paper-ready artifacts. (1) Generate Table 1 (main probing results), Table 2 (IIS matrix). (2) Generate Figures 1-4 using matplotlib (consistent color scheme, appropriate axis labels, statistical significance annotations). (3) Compute all correlations (H4: displacement consistency vs. holdout R²; H5: coupling vs. failure). (4) Write pilot_summary.md with decision outcomes (P1: framework validation result; P2: LoRA feasibility result). (5) Write brief text of key findings: confirmed/falsified hypotheses, effect sizes, negative results. Save all figures to exp/results/full/figures/ and all tables to exp/results/full/tables/.

visualizations.description: Table 1: Main results — probing R2 and CEM across all models and splits
